title: torilis infesta subsp. heterophylla
intro: Torilis infesta subsp. heterophylla, also known as wild chervil or horned poppy, is a plant species within the carrot family (Apiaceae) characterized by its biennial growth habit and distinctive horn-shaped leaflets. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can help alleviate digestive issues and reduce pain. The health benefits of Torilis infesta subsp. heterophylla include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
